introSection: Little Dipper is an Allan Herschell Company kiddie roller coaster located at Memphis Kiddie Park in Brooklyn, Ohio, USA. This is said to be the oldest operating steel roller coaster in North America. However, the Conneaut Lake Park Little Dipper is also said to have opened in 1950.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride is a standard Little Dipper model. It has a simple oval layout, allowing younger (and shorter) riders to enjoy the ride.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The original Allan Herschell train was replaced in the 1970s. The new train was built by Bradley & Kaye and was silver with blue accents. For the 2006 season, it was repainted red with white accents.

fullText:
Little Dipper is an Allan Herschell Company kiddie roller coaster located at Memphis Kiddie Park in Brooklyn, Ohio, USA. This is said to be the oldest operating steel roller coaster in North America. However, the Conneaut Lake Park Little Dipper is also said to have opened in 1950.

The ride is a standard Little Dipper model. It has a simple oval layout, allowing younger (and shorter) riders to enjoy the ride.

Black track and supports.



The original Allan Herschell train was replaced in the 1970s. The new train was built by Bradley & Kaye and was silver with blue accents. For the 2006 season, it was repainted red with white accents.
